title: Stories and Testimonials - Canada.ca
content:
## Stories and testimonials
Be inspired by others. Discover how Employment and Social Development  Canada programs and services are making a difference in the lives of people in Canada.

### Children and families
Parents and caregivers providing the best possible start in life for their children.
Learn  more about benefits for families .

### Youth (ages 12 to 30)
Youth in Canada on their paths to greatness.
Learn more about supports for youth in Canada .

### Job seekers and Workers
Canadians and newcomers pursuing their passions and careers, and the employers, organizations and programs helping them make it happen.
Learn more about skills and employment supports .

### Persons with disabilities
Stories of progress in building a more accessible and inclusive Canada.
Learn more about disability benefits .

### Older adults/seniors
Active seniors and the people, organizations, programs, and services supporting them.
Learn more about programs and services for seniors .
Individuals and organizations making a social impact in communities through Government of Canada funding, support and initiatives.
Learn more about social development programming .
Explore the impacts of Employment and Social Development Canada’s programs and services through the words and experiences of the real people and organizations in your community benefiting from them.
Informative articles, podcasts and videos.

- Manitoba: Supporting Early Childhood Educators Learn how Y-Winnipeg and Seven Oaks Child Day Care Centre is supporting early childhood educators through improved wages, training, and career pathways made possible by Canada‑wide early learning and child care investments. These supports are strengthening the workforce, expanding access for families, and enhancing the quality of child care across the community. Video Early Learning and Child Care

#### Manitoba: Supporting Early Childhood Educators
Learn how Y-Winnipeg and Seven Oaks Child Day Care Centre is supporting early childhood educators through improved wages, training, and career pathways made possible by Canada‑wide early learning and child care investments. These supports are strengthening the workforce, expanding access for families, and enhancing the quality of child care across the community.
- Early Learning and Child Care
- Mobilizing Francophone youth to help advance the Sustainable Development Goals When we invest in young people, we invest in the future. Réseau de développement économique et d'employabilité Prince Edward Island is reaching out to Francophone youth to raise awareness of green jobs and the SDGs, with impressive results. Article Sustainable Development Goals

#### Mobilizing Francophone youth to help advance the Sustainable Development Goals
When we invest in young people, we invest in the future. Réseau de développement économique et d'employabilité Prince Edward Island is reaching out to Francophone youth to raise awareness of green jobs and the SDGs, with impressive results.
- Sustainable Development Goals
- International Day of Families: Child Care That Supports Working Parents On International Day of Families, discover how the Canada‑wide early learning and child care system is making a difference for families by improving access to affordable, high-quality child care. Video Early Learning and Child Care

#### International Day of Families: Child Care That Supports Working Parents
On International Day of Families, discover how the Canada‑wide early learning and child care system is making a difference for families by improving access to affordable, high-quality child care.
- Early Learning and Child Care
- Meet The Running on Faith Project – a 2024 Canada’s Volunteer Awards recipient The Running on Faith Project, a 2024 recipient of Canada’s Volunteer Awards in the Social Innovation category, is using movement and community to support people in shelters and recovery centres across B.C. Founded by Andie van der Eerden, the program helps participants build confidence, improve mental health, and reconnect with purpose. Video Canada’s Volunteer Awards

#### Meet The Running on Faith Project – a 2024 Canada’s Volunteer Awards recipient
The Running on Faith Project, a 2024 recipient of Canada’s Volunteer Awards in the Social Innovation category, is using movement and community to support people in shelters and recovery centres across B.C. Founded by Andie van der Eerden, the program helps participants build confidence, improve mental health, and reconnect with purpose.
- Canada’s Volunteer Awards
- Learning beyond walls: Andrew Fleck Forest and Nature School At Andrew Fleck Forest and Nature School, educators use nature to support high‑quality early learning and meaningful play.  
Canada‑wide early learning and child care helps make programs like this possible, so every child can explore, discover, and grow. Video Early Learning and Child Care

#### Learning beyond walls: Andrew Fleck Forest and Nature School
At Andrew Fleck Forest and Nature School, educators use nature to support high‑quality early learning and meaningful play.  
Canada‑wide early learning and child care helps make programs like this possible, so every child can explore, discover, and grow.
- Early Learning and Child Care
- Improve Indigenous Early Learning & Child Care: Inuit Project. Learn how Shirley Tagalik, director at the Aqqiumavvik Society, partnered with Elders, parents, and Early Childhood Educators in Nunavut and Nunavik to strengthen Inuktitut language development for young children. Video Indigenous Early Learning and Child Care

#### Improve Indigenous Early Learning & Child Care: Inuit Project.
Learn how Shirley Tagalik, director at the Aqqiumavvik Society, partnered with Elders, parents, and Early Childhood Educators in Nunavut and Nunavik to strengthen Inuktitut language development for young children.
- Indigenous Early Learning and Child Care
- How Early Childhood Educators Keep French Vibrant in Canada At La Cité college, they know that French-language training is essential to preserving the Francophone identity and enriching our communities.  
Listen to instructors Pascale and Julie and students Natalie and Annabelle talk enthusiastically about their experiences in Early Childhood Education at La Cité. Video Early Learning and Child Care

#### How Early Childhood Educators Keep French Vibrant in Canada
At La Cité college, they know that French-language training is essential to preserving the Francophone identity and enriching our communities.  
Listen to instructors Pascale and Julie and students Natalie and Annabelle talk enthusiastically about their experiences in Early Childhood Education at La Cité.
- Early Learning and Child Care
- Y-Winnipeg: Child Care Making a Difference We’re celebrating Y Winnipeg and the positive impact their child care centres have on children and families. Affordable, high‑quality child care helps parents work, study, and thrive. Video Early Learning and Child Care

#### Y-Winnipeg: Child Care Making a Difference
We’re celebrating Y Winnipeg and the positive impact their child care centres have on children and families. Affordable, high‑quality child care helps parents work, study, and thrive.
- Early Learning and Child Care
- Ophélie Chiasson: Canada’s Volunteer Awards recipient Meet Ophélie Chiasson, a 2024 Canada’s Volunteer Awards recipient and a rising young leader from Atlantic Canada. As Vice-President of the Fédération des jeunes francophones du Nouveau-Brunswick, she helps create opportunities for learning, culture, and youth engagement. She was also named one of Acadie Nouvelle’s 30 rising Francophones under 30. Video Canada’s Volunteer Awards

#### Ophélie Chiasson: Canada’s Volunteer Awards recipient
Meet Ophélie Chiasson, a 2024 Canada’s Volunteer Awards recipient and a rising young leader from Atlantic Canada. As Vice-President of the Fédération des jeunes francophones du Nouveau-Brunswick, she helps create opportunities for learning, culture, and youth engagement. She was also named one of Acadie Nouvelle’s 30 rising Francophones under 30.
- Canada’s Volunteer Awards
- Andrew Fleck Educators Share Their Stories Why Being an Early Childhood Educator Matters Why being an early childhood educator matters, straight from the Andrew Fleck educators who live it every day.  
Hear their stories, their passion and the impact they make helping children feel safe, curious and ready to learn. Video Early Learning and Child Care

#### Andrew Fleck Educators Share Their Stories Why Being an Early Childhood Educator Matters
Why being an early childhood educator matters, straight from the Andrew Fleck educators who live it every day.  
Hear their stories, their passion and the impact they make helping children feel safe, curious and ready to learn.
- Early Learning and Child Care
- Meet Charlene: A Grandfriend Making a Difference at Andrew Fleck Children’s Services Watch as Charlene’s story shows how intergenerational programs at Andrew Fleck Children’s Services build connection and community between older adults and young children. Video Early Learning and Child Care

#### Meet Charlene: A Grandfriend Making a Difference at Andrew Fleck Children’s Services
Watch as Charlene’s story shows how intergenerational programs at Andrew Fleck Children’s Services build connection and community between older adults and young children.

- Mén̓mens ta smánit: A New Chapter for Families in Valleycliffe, BC Discover how Sea to Sky Community Services partnered with the Squamish Nation and the local community to open Mén̓mens ta smánit, a new child care centre in Valleycliffe, BC. Video Early Learning and Child Care

#### Mén̓mens ta smánit: A New Chapter for Families in Valleycliffe, BC
Discover how Sea to Sky Community Services partnered with the Squamish Nation and the local community to open Mén̓mens ta smánit, a new child care centre in Valleycliffe, BC.

- Jim Mulcahy: Thérèse Casgrain Lifelong Achievement Award 2024 Special Mention Meet Jim Mulcahy, a compassionate educator and storyteller, and a lifelong advocate whose generosity has touched lives far beyond his hometown of Antigonish, Nova Scotia. Video Canada’s Volunteer Awards

Meet Jim Mulcahy, a compassionate educator and storyteller, and a lifelong advocate whose generosity has touched lives far beyond his hometown of Antigonish, Nova Scotia.

Colleen Lussier, a 2025 Prime Minister’s Award recipient, is transforming early childhood education through play-based learning and inclusive spaces.

- What does a perfect space for a child to learn look like? Every back-to-school season brings a mix of excitement and nerves for parents. But one thing is constant: early childhood educators and teachers are always there to create enriching, supportive learning environments for children.

We asked the recipients of the Prime Minister’s Award for Excellence in Early Childhood Education to tell us how exactly. Video Early Learning and Child Care

#### What does a perfect space for a child to learn look like?
Every back-to-school season brings a mix of excitement and nerves for parents. But one thing is constant: early childhood educators and teachers are always there to create enriching, supportive learning environments for children.

We asked the recipients of the Prime Minister’s Award for Excellence in Early Childhood Education to tell us how exactly.
- Early Learning and Child Care
- Celebrating National Child Day 2025 Every child deserves high-quality, inclusive care that listens, supports, and empowers. This parent’s story from Seven Oaks Child Daycare Centre in Winnipeg shows what’s possible when families are supported and children with disabilities are welcomed with dignity and respect. Video Early Learning and Child Care

#### Celebrating National Child Day 2025
Every child deserves high-quality, inclusive care that listens, supports, and empowers. This parent’s story from Seven Oaks Child Daycare Centre in Winnipeg shows what’s possible when families are supported and children with disabilities are welcomed with dignity and respect.
- Early Learning and Child Care
- Inclusive Hiring As a young mom with an invisible disability working in a high-stress field, Emily struggled to achieve understanding, accommodations, and work-life balance. She found a supportive and inclusive workplace through the Opportunities Fund for Persons with Disabilities program. Video Opportunities Fund for Persons with Disabilities
As a young mom with an invisible disability working in a high-stress field, Emily struggled to achieve understanding, accommodations, and work-life balance. She found a supportive and inclusive workplace through the Opportunities Fund for Persons with Disabilities program.
- Opportunities Fund for Persons with Disabilities
- Women in the trades She took a chance on the trades, and now she’s building her future!  

Kallie always thought she’d become a nurse, but decided to try something else instead. Now, as a 2nd-year apprentice in plumbing at Kings Refrigeration & Air Conditioning Ltd, she’s breaking stereotypes, building a career she truly loves, and making plans for her future. Video Canadian Apprenticeship Strategy
She took a chance on the trades, and now she’s building her future!  

Kallie always thought she’d become a nurse, but decided to try something else instead. Now, as a 2nd-year apprentice in plumbing at Kings Refrigeration & Air Conditioning Ltd, she’s breaking stereotypes, building a career she truly loves, and making plans for her future.
- Canadian Apprenticeship Strategy
- Early Learning and Child Care - Adriana's story For working parents like Adriana, finding flexible child care isn’t just helpful, it’s essential. Dropping her child off in the morning and focusing on her day without worrying about the logistics? That’s the kind of tangible results the Canada-wide early learning and child care program is achieving for families. Video Early Learning and Child Care

#### Early Learning and Child Care - Adriana's story
For working parents like Adriana, finding flexible child care isn’t just helpful, it’s essential. Dropping her child off in the morning and focusing on her day without worrying about the logistics? That’s the kind of tangible results the Canada-wide early learning and child care program is achieving for families.
- Early Learning and Child Care
- Consider the skilled trades. Watch this inspiring story He went into the skilled trades, and it changed how he saw his future!  

If you’re good with your hands, eager to learn and ready to grow, skilled trades might be exactly what you need. Video Canadian Apprenticeship Strategy

#### Consider the skilled trades. Watch this inspiring story
He went into the skilled trades, and it changed how he saw his future!  

If you’re good with your hands, eager to learn and ready to grow, skilled trades might be exactly what you need.
- Canadian Apprenticeship Strategy
- Thank an older adult - Thank you, Denis! Inspired by his mother’s journey with Alzheimer’s, Denis brings joy to retirement homes through music. Even when memories fade, music remains, creating moments of comfort and connection. Video National Seniors Day

#### Thank an older adult - Thank you, Denis!
Inspired by his mother’s journey with Alzheimer’s, Denis brings joy to retirement homes through music. Even when memories fade, music remains, creating moments of comfort and connection.
- National Seniors Day
- Learn how these students got summer jobs that will help them in their future careers—and how you could too! The Government of Canada offered up to 76,000 jobs this year through the Canada Summer Jobs (CSJ) program. This makes it easier for employers to hire extra staff for the busy summer months, and young people are more likely to find jobs in their community to earn an income over the summer. Article Canada Summer Jobs

#### Learn how these students got summer jobs that will help them in their future careers—and how you could too!
The Government of Canada offered up to 76,000 jobs this year through the Canada Summer Jobs (CSJ) program. This makes it easier for employers to hire extra staff for the busy summer months, and young people are more likely to find jobs in their community to earn an income over the summer.
- Thank an older adult - Thank you, Ginette! In retirement, Ginette turned her vision into reality by starting a small farm; a project that has brought deep meaning to this new chapter of her life. She shares her love for animals with her grandchildren, passing down not only practical skills but also lifelong values. Video National Seniors Day

#### Thank an older adult - Thank you, Ginette!
In retirement, Ginette turned her vision into reality by starting a small farm; a project that has brought deep meaning to this new chapter of her life. She shares her love for animals with her grandchildren, passing down not only practical skills but also lifelong values.
- National Seniors Day
- Supporting seniors in the Maritimes Supported by the New Horizons for Seniors Program, the Sudanese Association of the Maritimes is making a profound impact on the lives of older adults in their community. The organization serves as a vital support network for newcomers and refugees from Sudan. Video New Horizons for Seniors Program

#### Supporting seniors in the Maritimes
Supported by the New Horizons for Seniors Program, the Sudanese Association of the Maritimes is making a profound impact on the lives of older adults in their community. The organization serves as a vital support network for newcomers and refugees from Sudan.
- New Horizons for Seniors Program
- Thank an older adult - Thank you, Simon! As an Anishnabee artist, Simon Brascoupé shares his culture through his art—bridging generations and celebrating the rich traditions of Indigenous knowledge. Video National Seniors Day

#### Thank an older adult - Thank you, Simon!
As an Anishnabee artist, Simon Brascoupé shares his culture through his art—bridging generations and celebrating the rich traditions of Indigenous knowledge.
- National Seniors Day
- Shaping Young Minds Through Play When children play in environments that reflect who they are, they feel free to explore, connect, and make lasting connections. 

Early Childhood Educators create inclusive spaces where  children feel seen, safe, and ready to learn. Video Early Learning and Child Care

When children play in environments that reflect who they are, they feel free to explore, connect, and make lasting connections.

- What accessibility means to Camp Misquah campers Through the Enabling Accessibility Fund’s small projects component, Camp Misquah has received over $140,000 to install accessibility features that enable all campers to create cherished memories each year that last a lifetime. Video Enabling Accessibility Fund

#### What accessibility means to Camp Misquah campers
Through the Enabling Accessibility Fund’s small projects component, Camp Misquah has received over $140,000 to install accessibility features that enable all campers to create cherished memories each year that last a lifetime.

- The Impact of Community Services Recovery Fund People First of Canada isn’t just an organization—they are a powerful advocate for the rights of people with intellectual disabilities.   When the pandemic left their members isolated, they used funds from the Community Services Recovery Fund to help them reconnect and emerge from the pandemic stronger. Video Community Services Recovery Fund

#### The Impact of Community Services Recovery Fund
People First of Canada isn’t just an organization—they are a powerful advocate for the rights of people with intellectual disabilities.   When the pandemic left their members isolated, they used funds from the Community Services Recovery Fund to help them reconnect and emerge from the pandemic stronger.
- Community Services Recovery Fund
- “We are proud to be Métis. Watch our nation rise again.” Métis Nation Saskatchewan is making it possible for a whole new generation to learn Michif and Dene and to grow up proud to be Métis. Video Indigenous Early Learning and Child Care

- My Little Métis Box During the Covid-19 pandemic, the Manitoba Métis Federation developed an incredible project to keep children and families connected to their culture and identity. A Red River Métis-centric educational box is delivered quarterly, full of materials that parents and children can explore and enjoy together. This is the story of the people behind the My Little Métis Box and the families who received it. Video Indigenous Early Learning and Child Care
During the Covid-19 pandemic, the Manitoba Métis Federation developed an incredible project to keep children and families connected to their culture and identity. A Red River Métis-centric educational box is delivered quarterly, full of materials that parents and children can explore and enjoy together. This is the story of the people behind the My Little Métis Box and the families who received it.
- Indigenous Early Learning and Child Care

The Yukon Aboriginal Women’s Council created the Elders in Residence project. This initiative offers at-risk women the opportunity to connect with Elders and engage in cultural activities such as sewing, crafting and beading.

Many youth hired by the camp through Canada Summer Jobs describe their coworkers as “family” and have felt inspired to come back year after year. Video Canada Summer Jobs

Camp Misquah is a residential camping experience for adults and children with special needs. Many youth hired by the camp through Canada Summer Jobs describe their coworkers as “family” and have felt inspired to come back year after year.

Short Breaks in Regina does just that! This video illustrates the impact of “Short Breaks” and how it extends to the whole community. Video New Horizons for Seniors Program

#### Supporting seniors in Regina

Swaraj’s journey with Canada Summer Jobs aligned with his love of sports: he used his marketing expertise to unite Canadians and promote healthy communities. He hopes to highlight the valuable role persons with disabilities can play in any workplace.
- Making a Difference – How Service Canada supported Ukrainian nationals Under the Canada-Ukraine authorization for emergency travel, launched in March 2022, more than 220,000 Ukrainian nationals arrived in Canada. Service Canada was there to help newcomers like Slava and Kateryna get the support they needed to start their new lives in Canada. Video Social Insurance Number

#### Making a Difference – How Service Canada supported Ukrainian nationals
Under the Canada-Ukraine authorization for emergency travel, launched in March 2022, more than 220,000 Ukrainian nationals arrived in Canada. Service Canada was there to help newcomers like Slava and Kateryna get the support they needed to start their new lives in Canada.

- Let’s Talk Future Skills podcast series The Future Skills podcast series explores the impact of technology, new business models, climate change, the COVID-19 pandemic and other factors on workforce trends and emerging in-demand skills. Podcast Future Skills

The Future Skills podcast series explores the impact of technology, new business models, climate change, the COVID-19 pandemic and other factors on workforce trends and emerging in-demand skills.
Our employees are dedicated to providing service excellence. Learn more about our service stories and how our work is supporting Canadians.

### Service Canada serves the people of Canada
Service Canada employees across the country are delivering important services to people across Canada, from issuing Social Insurance Numbers and passports to processing Employment Insurance claims and Old Age Security benefits.
Learn more about Service Canada’s services .

### Federally regulated workplaces are improving
The Labour Program is helping improve working conditions for federally regulated workers in Canada.
Learn more about the Labour Program .
